behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is core backoff math/policy logic on local inputs (failure count + cap), not a module handoff failure. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejected higher level: behavior text is only about "wait time ... doubles ... up to a configured maximum," which is pure calculation.
behaviors.test_description: Test the backoff function/object directly with consecutive failure counts and config cap, asserting sequence (e.g., 100, 200, 400, ...), and that values stop increasing past max.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure can occur at module boundary where HTTP response metadata (status/headers/body) is interpreted by retry policy. Integration is needed to verify handoff from transport wrapper to classifier/backoff logic. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network required if in-process stub transport returns realistic response objects; (b) no AI output; (c,d) no agentic chain. Rejected System: behavior is about identifying a response and computing delay, not real network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry middleware with a stub HTTP adapter that returns a 429-like response (including retry-after variants) and assert retry classifier marks it rate-limited and computed delay matches policy.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure is interaction among retry scheduler, request re-dispatch, and response propagation to caller. Unit tests of any one function can miss dropped/mutated request state. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) can be validated with fake clock + stub transport in-process; no mandatory real network/subprocess; (b,c,d) no AI/agentic steps. Rejected System: behavior text is transparent resend/return semantics, not OS/network timing pathologies.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake timers and a stub adapter sequence (429 then 200). Assert second call payload equals original request, is sent only after scheduled delay, and caller receives final success value without retry internals leaking.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this checks end-to-end retry loop termination plus error translation boundary (transport/retry failure -> public typed error). Unit-testing constructor/type alone misses propagation bugs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network required; deterministic stub failures suffice; (b,c,d) no AI/agentic dependency. Rejected System: behavior text focuses on typed error contract after retry count, not real infrastructure effects.
behaviors.test_description: Configure max retries N, stub N+1 rate-limit failures, execute `call()`, and assert rejection is `QuotaExhaustedError` (type + relevant fields) and not raw adapter/generic error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears in orchestrated state transitions across retry controller + quota tracker + termination guard; single-function tests miss loop conditions. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) can be proven with in-process stubs/fake timers; no required real OS/network/subprocess; (b,c,d) no AI/agentic chain. Rejected System: behavior explicitly concerns logical termination of retry chain, which is reproducible without real infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Run `call()` with persistent rate-limit responses, bounded max retries, fake clock advancement, and assert finite number of attempts plus final surfaced error within test timeout (no unbounded loop).

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is shared-state coordination across concurrent callers/modules (client instances/call sites + quota store). Unit tests of quota math alone miss cross-caller synchronization bugs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) behavior text says "same process"; no real network/OS prerequisite; (b,c,d) no AI/agentic dependency. Rejected System: concrete text "same process" disqualifies need for external infra to catch core failure.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate shared quota manager with multiple concurrent `call()` invocations (via Promise.all) using stub transport; assert combined consumption follows one global bucket and later callers see exhausted/throttled state consistently.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bugs emerge at queue + dispatcher interaction under async scheduling; a unit test of queue data structure alone can miss dispatch race behavior. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network needed; in-process scheduler + stub transport is sufficient; (b,c,d) no AI/agentic dependency. Rejected System: behavior is FIFO dispatch semantics, reproducible with deterministic local timing controls.
behaviors.test_description: Enqueue multiple blocked requests, release capacity one slot at a time, and assert observed send order matches submission order exactly (FIFO) across async boundaries.
